text:
| `idref.rs:112` `looks_like_codename` gate in `extract_mentions` | Accept any agent-slug-shaped token (`[A-Za-z0-9._-]`, optionally `@host`). Let `resolve_recipient` decide validity. This is the one real edit: the gate must stop filtering to NATO-codename shape. | [...] | `chat_write.rs:99-103` "must name a concrete session codename" bail | Drop the `target_session` requirement. Route by `target.pubkey` alone — delivery (line 176) already keys on `mentioned_pubkey`. |
| `chat_write.rs:300-308` `find_session_by_codename` step | Remove. The bare-slug step (309-318) + `resolve_agent_pubkey` covers `@haiku`/`@haiku1`. |
| `chat_write.rs:333-348` `find_session_by_codename` fn | Delete (no other caller — confirmed by grep). | [...] - **Targeting** — relax the mention gate in `idref.rs` to accept `@haiku`/`@haiku1`; delete `find_session_by_codename` + the "must name a concrete session codename" bail in `chat_write.rs`. `resolve_agent_pubkey("haiku1", host)` already resolves the label→selected-pubkey (each instance publishes kind:0 under its display label), so this is mostly deletion. [...] - `extract_mentions` gate relaxed: instead of NATO-codename shape it now keeps a `[A-Za-z0-9._-]+` run optionally host-qualified as `label@host`, with trailing `.`/`@` trimmed. (A concurrent intentional refinement also skips `@` preceded by an alphanumeric, so emails aren't mentions — coexists fine.) [...] - Removed the hard `bail!` requiring a mention to name a concrete session. A mention token is now resolved via `resolve_recipient`; on `Ok` it sets the mention, on `Err` (unresolvable) it is silently treated as no-mention (chat is never blocked). The project/work-root mismatch guard is preserved.
- Added `mentioned_label` (the raw resolved token) to the mention tuple and the RPC response; `mentioned_session` is now `Option` (None for label mentions, which route purely by pubkey). [...] - Confirmation line changed from `mentioning session {codename}` to `mentioning @{label}` driven by the RPC's new `mentioned_label`; falls back to plain `sent chat {id}` when no mention.
 [...] - **Targeting**: `@haiku`/`@haiku1` mentions resolve to the selected pubkey via `resolve_agent_pubkey`; `find_session_by_codename` + the "must name a concrete session codename" bail removed.
- **whoami removed entirely**; self-identity folded into `who` (a `self` block + header).
